zh.title: Solana/美元技术分析：窄幅震荡偏多整理
zh.content:
当前SOL/美元呈窄幅震荡上行结构，价格稳定运行于73美元上方，短线多空力量均衡，但略偏多头，整体趋势仍处于整理后的温和上行阶段。

MACD柱值维持在0.70上方显示动能偏多，价格站稳20日均线72.40之上，短期均线支撑有效，但RSI缺失使超买判断有限整体偏强。

下方支撑关注72.96与72.40均线区域，上方阻力集中于73.14前高，若放量突破或将上看73.50至74美元区间震荡为主。

短线建议以区间高抛低吸为主，未放量突破前避免追涨，可在72.4附近分批布局多单，突破73.14再顺势加仓控制风险，执行纪律

en.title: Solana vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
Solana against the US Dollar is currently in a tight consolidation phase with a mild bullish bias. Price action at 73.12 sits just above the 20-day SMA of 72.405, suggesting short-term support is being respected. The 24-hour range is extremely compressed between 72.96 and 73.14, indicating low volatility and market indecision. Overall structure leans slightly upward, but momentum remains weak and requires confirmation through a breakout.

From an indicator standpoint, the MACD reading at 0.701 indicates positive momentum building beneath the surface, though the absence of a visible signal line limits crossover interpretation. RSI data is unavailable, reducing clarity on overbought or oversold conditions. However, price stability above the SMA(20) generally supports a neutral-to-bullish stance. The lack of strong directional movement suggests accumulation rather than distribution at current levels.

Key support is established at the intraday low of 72.96, with stronger structural support near the SMA(20) at 72.40. A break below this zone could expose a deeper retracement toward the 71.80 area. On the upside, immediate resistance lies at 73.14, the current range high, followed by a psychological extension zone around 73.50 to 74.00. A decisive break above resistance would be required to confirm bullish continuation.

Trading conditions favor range-based strategies in the short term, given the compressed volatility. Scalpers may look to buy near 72.96–72.40 support and take quick profits near 73.14 resistance. Swing traders should wait for a confirmed breakout above 73.14 with volume expansion before entering long positions. Conversely, a breakdown below the SMA would shift bias toward short setups targeting lower support zones.
